title: axe-core Browser Scans, Interaction States, and Manual Accessibility Evidence · Changed-Schema Lab
summary: An independent municipal service owner supplies a different origin, markup family, dynamic boundary, accessibility need, resource budget, and injected failure and transfers axe-core browser scans, interaction states, and manual accessibility evidence into a changed stateful automated-plus-manual accessibility review. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of stateful automated-plus-manual accessibility review with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress thro…
objectives: Run axe-core with declared version, rules, tags, inclusions, exclusions, page state, and complete result evidence.
Scan initial, navigation, dialog, form error, authenticated, loading, empty, and changed responsive states reached through user actions.
Preserve rule, impact, targets, HTML snippet digest, help, occurrence grouping, false-positive review, owner, and retest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Axe Rule Scope
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab TypeScript team handles case 8972 during an origin-fairness and event-loop capacity rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove versioned axe scans across interaction states plus triage, manual coverage, and regression evidence; URL, robots, body, parser, frontier, privacy, accessibility, and report budgets stay explicit; then change one HTML repair, Cheerio mode, selector, unknown value, record schema, link relation, or metadata case and retain rejected evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Run axe-core with declared version, rules, tags, inclusions, exclusions, page state, and complete result evidence.
